# Tamorsenasiaa

*A world generated by Cartogenesis v0.11.0 from seed `mistral`. Regenerate it exactly with `node src/cli.ts generate --seed mistral`.*

## Overview

- **Extent:** 360 × 360 cells
- **Land / ocean:** 45.1% land, 54.9% ocean, 2 lake(s)
- **Climate & life:** dominant biome **Grassland**, 16 biomes in all
- **Peoples:** 30 regions, 18 settlements, 3 realms
- **Capital:** Nasida, seat of House Redenabi
- **Dominant power:** Brabravo (of 3 surviving realm(s))
- **The present year:** 1119 After Reckoning

## Notable features

- **Mount Mausiassoau** — the highest peak
- **The Vintememorbe** — the greatest river
- **Lake Rabolenre** — the largest inland water

- Thauknaskvo is a coast of windswept scrub, its folk hardy and sea-wise.

## Notable figures

- **Licemorlivento, the General** — Licemorlivento broke the long siege of Paupoa.
- **Bemaa, the Mountaineer** — Bemaa was first to stand atop Mausiassoau and return alive.
- **Meovemorba, the Explorer** — Meovemorba charted the far coasts and traced the Vintememorbe to its source.
- **Oleriamo, the Scholar** — Oleriamo mapped the waters of Rabolenre.
- **Mauciareo, the Architect** — Mauciareo raised the great road between Nasida and Epavento.

- Epavento (port, trade hub, rich) — Timber, Grain, Livestock, Fish
- Paupoa (port, prosperous) — Livestock, Grain, Wine

## Faiths

### the Faith of Dasto — Dasto, god of the Forest

> Dasto planted the first tree in Thauknaskvo and hides still among the deep woods.

Followed across 8 region(s).

### the Way of Temi — Temi, god of the Storm

> Temi rides the storms down the Vintememorbe, and Egha both fears and blesses the rain.

Followed across 16 region(s).

### the Way of Dariamiabau — Dariamiabau, god of War

> Dariamiabau forged the first blade in the fires of Mausiassoau and gave it to the bold of Reriandovi.

Followed across 5 region(s).

### the Cult of Syienaeywyneleo — Syienaeywyneleo, god of the Sun

> Each dawn Syienaeywyneleo drives the sun over Mausiassoau; the people of Yilnela greet it with fire.

Followed across 1 region(s).

## Resources & trade

- **Major exports:** Grain, Livestock, Horses, Wine, Timber
- **Wealthiest settlement:** Nasida
- **Trade hubs:** Nasida, Epavento, Lentelo
- **Deposits:** Iron ×26, Copper ×19, Gold ×8, Gems ×5, Stone ×51, Coal ×19, Timber ×12, Grain ×45, Livestock ×123, Fish ×11, Furs ×3, Spices ×3, Salt ×10, Horses ×38, Wine ×22

## Legends of the founding age

- **41 After Reckoning** — The city of Nasida was founded on a sheltered harbour, destined to become the seat of power.
- **65 After Reckoning** — The city of Epavento was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **75 After Reckoning** — The city of Paupoa was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **76 After Reckoning** — Nasida rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Tamorsenasiaa was proclaimed.
- **86 After Reckoning** — Epavento rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Econadau was proclaimed.
- **114 After Reckoning** — Paupoa rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Pamialenbeomar was proclaimed.
- **567 After Reckoning** — An academy founded on the shores of Rabolenre gathered the age's finest cartographers.
- **744 After Reckoning** — Under a long peace, Nasida flourished; its libraries and roads became the wonder of the world.
- **1064 After Reckoning** — Mausiassoau woke in fire and ash; a hard winter followed, and harvests failed across the land.
- **1072 After Reckoning** — A great storm-tide flooded Nasida; the lower quarters were lost to the sea.
